rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HATSUME DORNECKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Petitioner requests distribution to Decedent's siblings as the "personal representatives" of Fumiko Tanaka of an approximate amount of $850,000. The Japanese statutes provided appear incomplete (e.g. Article 889 does not provide order of rank, and Article 900 are missing (i)-(iii) language) and a relevant code section is missing (i.e. Article 889 refer to Article 887, but Article 887 is not provided). Moreover, the code sections cited do not show that heirs become the "personal representatives". Court to determine whether sufficient legal authority has been provided to grant distribution as requested. 2) Attorney is requesting extraordinary fees at $625/hr for services provided, including for services provided prior to appointment and for ordinary services performed prior to the discovery of Decedent's will. 3) Petitioner requests authority to pay a creditor's claim filed by her attorney in the amount of $625 for 1 hour spent on an initial client meeting with Decedent to discuss drafting of an updated will. It does not appear that a will was ever drafted and no substantiation has been provided showing that Decedent agreed to pay this amount. 4) Petitioner requests authority to pay a creditor's claim she filed for $125 for the preparation of Decedent's income taxes for 2016. The invoice for her services is dated 10/15/17, which was after Decedent's death (3/5/17) and before her appointment (10/11/17). It is unclear how she had the authority to provide these services. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES C. GRIMES REVOCABLE INTERVIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/15/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) An allegation required by Probate Code section 1064(a)(5) is missing. 2) Schedule C shows the petitioner's purchase of appliances, but they are not listed as assets on Schedules E & F (or a schedule required by section 1063(b)). 3) Many disbursement items do not show check numbers. What payment method did the petitioner use? 4) An account statement confirming the ending cash balance must be filed. 5) The conservatee's car distributed to the petitioner is not listed as an asset on Schedule E & F (or a section 1063(b) schedule). 6) Does the trust have any interest in the boilmakers pension? 7) The petitioner's timesheets show he spent 16 hours on "Schedule Citi Acct" on 03/29/2019 & 04/01/2019. What was the purpose? More specific descriptions are needed. 8) How did the petitioner confirm the updated addresses of Curtis Grimes and Willy Tupuola listed in the proof of service? 9) Should notice be given to the trustees and beneficiaries named in the superseded trust in light of the pending petition to invalidate the restated trust? 10) What is the current balance of cash held by the trust?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILMA L. DANIELS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Order The Immediate Sale Of The Real Property Of The Trust; Empower Co-Trustee And Co-Beneficiary Joe Dudley Jones, Iii To Have The Sole
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The sole issue for this hearing is whether this matter should be taken off calendar due to counsel's failure to provide courtesy copies. No argument on the merits will be heard. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PERETZ-CHAMI FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/6/20. A guardian ad litem must be appointed to represent the minor heirs (presumably, the beneficiaries). A full copy of the trust must be filed to show who the beneficiaries are (the specific provisions regarding the amounts to be distributed may be redacted). The correct form of notice is Judicial Council form DE-115. A copy of the petition must be served along with the notice of hearing. Copies of statements (redacted for any personal information such as SSN) must be filed to show how title is vested in the accounts at issue. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/25/20. If a guardian ad litem cannot be appointed in time to receive notice of the petition and supplement to the petition (a copy of which must also be served on all interested parties), Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request a further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ZHANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for information on appearing by CourtCall and see below for Zoom information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Issue: The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner's notes. a. Waivers of bond are not on the mandatory DE-142 judicial council form. b. Petitioner is a nonresident and the Petition does not address Local Rule 14.37. c. Decedent's parents have priority to serve and have not filed declinations to serve and have not filed nominations of Petitioner. d. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities DE-147 judicial council form. e. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES RICHARD TOOMBS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/15/2020. 1) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) As the petitioner is a nonresident of California, a bond will be required despite waivers from all beneficiaries. The minimum bond amount is $20,000 even if no liabilities or claims are anticipated. See Local Rule of San Francisco (LRSF) 14.37. B) Item 6b of the petition was not checked. C) Item 9 of the petition must be completed. D) The names and dates of death of the decedent's predeceased siblings who were the parents of Beverly Jean Nelson Stone and Fred Allen Nelson Jr. must be listed in item 8 of the petition. 2) Notice must be given to Tommy Groves at his place of business or residence. Probate Code section 1220(a)(3).
